assertions.description: indexedDB is exposed on the window object.
indexedDB.open is a function returning an IDBOpenDBRequest.
IDBObjectStore is exposed so authors can detect object-store features.
IDBTransaction is exposed so transaction durability semantics can be observed.
Opening a transient database round-trips a Date through structured clone, which the SQLite backend must preserve.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const name='__conf_idb_probe_'+Math.random().toString(36).slice(2);const req=indexedDB.open(name,1);return await new Promise((resolve)=>{req.onupgradeneeded=()=>{req.result.createObjectStore('s');};req.onsuccess=()=>{const db=req.result;const tx=db.transaction('s','readwrite');const store=tx.objectStore('s');const d=new Date('2026-01-15T10:00:00Z');store.put(d,'k');tx.oncomplete=()=>{const rtx=db.transaction('s','readonly');const r=rtx.objectStore('s').get('k');r.onsuccess=()=>{const ok=r.result instanceof Date && r.result.getTime()===d.getTime();db.close();indexedDB.deleteDatabase(name);resolve(ok);};};req.onerror=()=>resolve(false);};});})()

assertions.description: The database round-trips a Blob payload, which exercises the SQLite backend's BLOB storage path.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const name='__conf_idb_blob_'+Math.random().toString(36).slice(2);const req=indexedDB.open(name,1);return await new Promise((resolve)=>{req.onupgradeneeded=()=>{req.result.createObjectStore('s');};req.onsuccess=()=>{const db=req.result;const tx=db.transaction('s','readwrite');const store=tx.objectStore('s');const b=new Blob(['hello']);store.put(b,'k');tx.oncomplete=()=>{const rtx=db.transaction('s','readonly');const r=rtx.objectStore('s').get('k');r.onsuccess=async()=>{const ok=r.result instanceof Blob && (await r.result.text())==='hello';db.close();indexedDB.deleteDatabase(name);resolve(ok);};};req.onerror=()=>resolve(false);};});})()
